gradeRationale: The only supplied violation is a resolved, non-health-based public reporting rule violation. No health-based contaminant violation is provided, but the limited data do not support an A grade or a complete assessment of current water quality.
summary: Hoboken Water Utility had a Consumer Confidence Report Rule violation beginning July 1, 2021; the EPA data mark it as resolved. This was a public reporting violation rather than a health-based contaminant exceedance, and no contaminant test results are supplied.

contaminants.name: Consumer Confidence Report Rule
contaminants.whatItIs: A public reporting rule listed in the supplied EPA data; the violation is marked resolved with status “R.”
contaminants.healthEffects: The supplied data classify this violation as non-health-based and identify no associated health effect.

filterRecommendation.reason: The supplied record shows no health-based contaminant exceedance, so it does not support recommending a treatment filter. This does not establish that every possible contaminant was tested or absent.

fish.explanation: Fish safety cannot be determined from this violation record. The disinfectant type—chlorine versus chloramine—and water-quality measurements are unavailable.

plants.explanation: Plant safety cannot be determined because hardness, salinity, and contaminant measurements are unavailable.

pets.explanation: Pet safety cannot be fully assessed because no contaminant concentrations or current water-quality measurements are supplied.
